microcrystalline cellulose.Notes: Good compressibility; commonly used in direct compression tablets.

lactose.Notes: Common filler; check Maillard reaction risk with amine APIs.

crospovidone.Notes: Insoluble, rapid swelling; useful for immediate-release tablets.

sodium starch glycolate.Notes: Swelling disintegrant; excessive amount may slow dissolution.

hydroxypropyl cellulose.Notes: Used in wet granulation and film coating.

low substituted hydroxypropyl cellulose.Notes: Used in tablets as a disintegrant, binder, and compression aid.

low substituted hpc.Notes: Low-substituted hydroxypropyl cellulose is commonly used as a disintegrant and binder.

hypromellose.Notes: Used in coating and modified-release matrix tablets.

magnesium stearate.Notes: Over-lubrication may reduce dissolution and tablet hardness.

sodium stearyl fumarate.Notes: Alternative lubricant with lower hydrophobicity than magnesium stearate.

talc.Notes: Used in tablets and coating systems.

polyethylene glycol.Notes: Used as plasticizer in film coating; may improve wettability or solubilization.

peg.Notes: PEG is commonly used as a plasticizer in film coating and may improve wettability or solubilization.

glyceryl behenate.Notes: Used as lubricant, release modifier, or lipid matrix former.

hydrogenated castor oil.Notes: Used as lubricant and hydrophobic matrix/release modifying excipient.

pregelatinized starch.Notes: Used as filler, binder, and disintegrant in tablet formulations.
